choices: Agree to get the shot
Ask for more information before getting the shot
Ask why she needs a shot
Decline the shot
Probably should get parent's permission before getting random shots
1.content:
This was it. This was Samantha's new life for the next few years. No matter how much she had protested and fought her parents, the fact was, she was here now. There was no going back. She didn't think that she'd be able to make it on her own, and her parents had been very clear that they wouldn't support her if she did.

She'd threatened to leave. To cut all ties with them. She'd cried and wept when she realized she couldn't win. None of her friends had been willing to help, willing to take her in. And so, she left. She'd left her old life behind, and made her way to the the ends of the Earth, destined to be forgotten. Or at least that's what it seemed like.

Where once her life had been filled with skyscrapers and concrete, now it held small buildings and forest in every direction. Her old school had held over a thousand students, and now she wasn't sure if there were a thousand people in this lonely village. She was probably exaggerating to herself about the size of the town, but still, the town might as well be empty for all she cared. None of her friends were here. None of the places she knew, or had grown up with. All for what? Because her dad had to get moved here for his work.

It wasn't like he had an important job or anything. He could have easily found a better job. One with prestige. One where she wouldn't be forced to move to this forgotten land. Honestly, she didn't even know what her dad did. He had worked with a pharmacy company, but he had never really talked about what he did. Well, he still worked for them, but now it was in the middle of nowhere.

The sound of a bell ringing caused Samantha to quicken her pace as she made her way up the lonely sidewalk towards a small, brick building with the words Bountiful High School in tarnished copper letters stuck to the front.

She had almost walked right past her new school, unused to something so small. Her last high school had been a massive, state-of-the-art building. This more reminded her of a small medical clinic in the horror movies she loved to watch. She was reminded even more of a horror movie as she made her way through the creaking front door, which echoed in the cold, empty hallways.

Shivering at how cold it was inside, she jumped slightly as the door slammed shut behind her with a loud crack. She glanced around, and let out a sigh of relief that no one had seen her jump. Catching her breath, she glanced around her new school with an obvious look of disappointment.

The floor was nothing more than a plain white tile, that merged seamlessly with matching colored walls. Though the paint was in desperate need of being repainted, and there were obvious cracks in the floor. The puke colored lockers didn't do much to break up how plain the inside of the building was, though she probably should've expected something like this with how run down the outside had appeared.

She pulled out her phone, cursing under her breath as she noticed the time. At her old school she'd always been early to school. She'd been a model student that the teachers loved. Now, she couldn't even show up to class on time. Glancing back and forth, she began searching for her first class, hearing voices coming from many of the closed doors that broke up the rows of lockers. Finally, finding her class, she slowly opened the door, and stepped inside and offered a hasty apology to the plump teacher at the front of the room before taking a seat in one of the empty chairs next to a really overweight boy who looked like he was struggling to reach his desk because of how big he was. The teacher made a brief comment about her being a new student to the class, but she didn't really pay attention to what he was saying.

She did her best to pay attention to what the teacher was talking about, but just couldn't seem to stay focused on the lesson at hand. She let out a sigh of relief as the bell finally rang, dismissing them to go to their next class. She jumped up before anyone else, and quickly went to find her next class. She finally found it just before the bell rang and she took a seat in-between another couple of fat students. The next class went in much the same way as the first. And the third was almost identical to the first two.

Finally, the bell rang for lunch, and she jumped up, rushing past a girl who seemed so fat that Samantha was surprised she could even walk. She was one of the first ones to the lunch room, which surprised her. Usually, there was already a huge line at her old school. Hopefully, they would have good food here. That was one of her complaints about her old school, was that it always seemed like they were serving cardboard. On top of that, they never gave enough food and she always left lunch hungry.

Sam grabbed a tray and moved towards the serving area, where a couple of very large ladies were waiting for her.

"I don't recognize you. You must be the new girl." Said the first lady with a large smile as she heaped a massive portion of potatoes and gravy onto Sam's plate.

"Yeah." Sam mumbled.

The woman chuckled as she loaded a huge portion of roast beef onto Sam's tray, "Oh dearie, there's no need to be shy, we won't bite. If you ever need anything just let us know."

Sam mumbled an okay and finished getting her food, feeling her tray growing noticeably heavier with each item. She couldn't believe how much food was on her tray, and just kept shaking her head as she plopped down at one of the tables in the corner, where she hopefully wouldn't be disturbed.

She watched as the other students began arriving and lining up as she shoveled the delicious food into her eager mouth. She hadn't realized how hungry she'd been, but now that she was eating she couldn't stop herself despite the massive portions. Though, if this was the norm, it was no wonder why almost every kid in line was at least chubby, if not incredibly obese. She really was going to have to be careful or she very well might join them.

She was surprised when she discovered that she'd eaten the entire tray of food, and promised herself that she'd go for a run later that evening. Which reminded her that she still hadn't heard anything about any of the sports teams here. Though, now that she thought about it, she hadn't seen anything advertising any sports. She didn't recall seeing any trophies in any of the display cases, or fliers on the walls. She hadn't even seen a gym since arriving.

She scoffed. The school was probably too small to really warrant any activities like that, though from the look of the other students, it might not be a bad idea. Maybe she would have to talk with the principal about starting up a team or a club. The principal would probably be happy that she was showing some initiative to help the other students. Still, she was the new girl here, so it wasn't like she really knew much about this place yet. Maybe they did have a team. She resolved herself to investigate it further once she was more settled in.

Getting up from the table seemed a little harder after her massive lunch, and she renewed her promise to go running after school so she wouldn't get fat. She tugged on her shorts as they felt like they were cutting into her bloated belly. She couldn't believe how much food she'd eaten. She'd have to tell the lunch ladies to take it easy in the future.

As she was just about to leave the lunch room, she heard someone call her name. She glanced around curiously, and spotted a rather large woman in a billowing dress striding towards her, "Samantha? Samantha Hatchins?"

"Yeah?"

"So nice to meet you!" said the woman with a large smile as she thrust her hand out and gripped Sam's free hand tightly, "I'm Mrs. Vee, the school nurse. If I can just have a moment of your time, it seems like some of your medical information is incomplete and I just need you to come to my office so we can fill in the gaps!"

"Ok." Sam said, feeling like her arm had been yanked out of its socket as Mrs. Vee began practically dragging her down the hallway.

"Now, I hope everything has been going well on your first day. I know we kinda threw you to the wolves, but I hope you've been able to find all your classes and everyone is making you feel welcome. We don't get many knew people in town, most people don't even know this place exists, but you'll find this is a very tight-knit community and we look after each other."

Sam struggled to keep up with Mrs. Vee's rapid speech as she seemed to go into detail about everything about the town before they made it to her office. Her head was spinning as Mrs. Vee half dragged her into a small room that looked like a cozy patients room, complete with a sunflower in a vase by the only bed.

"Have a seat." Mrs. Vee said, gesturing to the bed.

Sam cautiously sat down on the side of the bed, facing Mrs. Vee as she sat down in a large chair and grabbed a clipboard. The next half hour went by rather quickly as Sam was forced to answer question after question about her medical history. Most of the questions were pretty easy, though there were a few that she found a little bit prying.

"Well I think that's all my questions, but before you go, I need to give you a booster shot. It just has some vitamins and other nutrients to make sure you stay healthy."
1.title: New Girl, New Life

choices: Agree to go to the buffet with him
Agree, but suggest a fast food place
Agree and go to his home
Decline, go home alone and get some food
Decline, go home and go for a run, you don't want to end up like him
11.content:
Sam could hear the light ticking of a clock, as she glanced around the room trying to locate it. She'd already heard the first bell, signaling the end of lunch, which meant she probably had less than five minutes to get her shot and find her class. That really didn't leave a whole lot of time for questions if she wanted to be to class on time.

She bit her lip as she glanced from the door to Mrs. Vee, who was waiting patiently for Sam to respond. Normally she would like to ask more questions about this shot she was about to get, but she really didn't have time, and she was sure that refusing would probably waste more time than the questions. Besides, she had already been late to one of her classes, and she wasn't eager to be known as the girl who was always late.

Sam sighed, nodding her head as she lifted her left sleeve for the nurse.

"This won't hurt a bit," Mrs. Vee said, pulling out a syringe filled with clear liquid.

Sam instinctively looked away as the shot approached. It wasn't like she was afraid of shots, but she was positive that they hurt less if you didn't watch them. Though a moment later, she felt a sharp prick, followed by a burning sensation as the nurse injected her with whatever was in the syringe.

"There, that wasn't so bad." Mrs Vee said, slapping a tan bandage onto the site of the shot.

Sam just shook her head as she grabbed her bag and stood up. She grimaced at the burning sensation in her arm as the weight of her bag made her shoulder feel a bit sore.

Mrs. Vee smiled as she hoisted her large body to her feet and moved to open the door for Sam, letting in the noise of people trying to get to class on time, "Well, you better hurry if you don't want to be late for class."

Sam just nodded before stepping out into the rapidly emptying hallway, still moving her arm as she tried to work out the burning sensation. She glanced over her shoulder as she heard the door close with a loud click behind her, and began making her way towards where she though her class was.

Despite how small the school was, she still got turned around a couple of times, before finding her next class. She hurried in to find everyone already at their seats, and plopped down in an empty chair right as the bell rang. Breathing a sigh of relief, she quickly forgot about the shot as she focused on paying attention in class. As the day went on, though, she found herself growing really hungry despite her large lunch, and by the time she took her seat in her last class it was almost all she could think about.

Her stomach let out a massive growl that was masked by the ringing of the final bell of the day. She blushed slightly, hoping that no one had noticed as she quickly gather her stuff. She was determined to get some food. Though, she probably should work out first, lest she end up like her fellow classmates.

She was almost to the door, when she felt someone tap her shoulder. She turned to see an extremely fat boy standing behind her, looking sheepishly at the ground, while he was obviously blushing.

"I, uh, was wondering if you wanted to maybe grab a bite to eat. I'd be happy to show you around town a little since, you're new. I know a really good buffet, though my mom makes really good food too." the boy stammered, obviously trying to be nice, despite being really nervous.

Sam was rather surprised that this was the first student to talk to her all day. Especially since he seemed like a bit of an outcast, even among his fellow students. Still, despite how large he was, there was a bit of a boyish cuteness to him, and she was sure he'd probably be one of the most handsome students if he lost a couple hundred pounds. On top of that, it wasn't like anyone else had really gone out of their way to befriend her.
11.title: No Time Like the Present

choices: Agree, and say that she wants to join her sister in gaining weight
Agree, and offer to help her sister gain weight
Agree to keep her secret, but stay distant, she doesn't want to get fat.
111.content:
Sam glanced from the massive boy to the door, then back to the boy who was shifting uncomfortably as he awaited her response. Despite how fat the boy was, there was something about him that she was drawn to. Though it probably helped that she was starving.

"Sure, that sounds nice." she said, smiling as she watched his face light up with glee, "I could definitely go or some food."

"Great! You'll love it!" the boy replied, as he began following her towards the door, "Oh, I'm Stephen, by-the-way."

"Samantha."

Samantha found it hard to walk at the slow pace that Stephen was setting, but it wasn't like she could blame him with how large he was. Though, she was finding herself wondering what it would be like to be as fat as him as they made their way through the small town. Despite how chilly the air was, Stephen seemed to be sweating before too long, though he had plenty of insulation on him to keep him warm. Especially since one of his arms was probably as thick as both her legs together.

Sam shivered, pulling her jacket tighter around herself as she kicked at some leaves on the ground, though she could still hear Stephen's panting over the crumpling of the leaves.

"Yeah, there really isn't too much to this town except a few restaurants and a movie theater. Though, I guess if you like hiking, there are plenty of trails in the forest, though I generally just like to play video games since I don't have anyone to hang out with. Do you like video games?"

Samantha chuckled, "I used to play them a lot, but I guess I ended up on the opposite end as you. None of my friends wanted to play video game, so I ended up on a few of the sports teams. Though it was probably a good thing, since I was pretty chubby when I was a child. Are there any teams at school?"

"Not really. No one really is interested in sports here."

"Really? That's weird."

Now it was Stephen's turn to chuckle, "I don't know if you noticed, but everyone here isn't really what you call athlete material."

"Well maybe if they had a sports team or two, that would change."

"We had one once, but no one would ever sign up for it. Actually, we might still have a team, but you'd probably have to talk to the principal. Though I don't think it would do you much good. Everyone in town is fat."

"Yeah, I noticed. You don't have any health nuts or anything here?"

"Well, it is a pretty small town, but it just ends up that everyone is fat"

"What about people who move here, like me. I'm not fat. Though, the rest of my family probably would fit in pretty well."

"Do you have any siblings?"

"Just a younger sister, she's 14. Then my mom and dad, obviously. Though you didn't really answer my question."

Stephen kicked at a small pile of leaves as he plodded heavily along, "Like I said. Everyone in town is fat. It's just how it is."

"Well, I don't plan on getting fat." Sam said, though part of her wondered if she really meant it.

It was true what she'd said to Stephen, she really had been pretty chubby as a child. Honestly, she hadn't really minded being on the heavier side, but once she started playing sports she'd been able to get in shape pretty quickly. However, even then she hadn't really cared if she was thin or not, it just kind of happened. On top of that, it was hard for her to deny the fact that she was getting aroused thinking about how fat everyone was.

"Here we are." Said Stephen, placing a hand on Sam's shoulder.

They were standing outside a rather large restaurant with brownish brick that made it look pretty plain. Still, the crowd of people was hard to argue with. Sam's stomach let out a rumble as a delicious smell wafted to them on the cool breeze. Drawn almost on instinct, they found a seat and began eating. Sam couldn't believe how hungry she was as she went back for plate after plate of food, all the while talking and laughing with Stephen. There was just something about the delicious food and good company that let her cut loose and eat until she was absolutely stuffed.

She sat back on the bench, looking at the pile of plates on the table, "Whew, I had no idea I could eat so much."

"Yeah, I'm stuffed." Replied Stephen from the other side of the table. She was honestly surprised he could even fit at the booth as he almost took up the entire side to himself. Though, she found her thoughts wandering to what it would be like to be that big.

"So does anyone get so fat they can't leave their house?" Sam suddenly blurted out, though she quickly felt her face go red at being so blunt.

"Honestly, there's probably more people who are immobile than those who aren't. School even has a special program where they take homework to all the kids who can't make it to school because they're so big. I've, uh, actually considered signing up for it a couple of times, though if I had, I wouldn't have met you. Anyway, I guess I should probably let you get going so your family doesn't get worried."

After a few quick goodbyes, Sam began making her way home as Stephen waddled off in the opposite direction. It was definitely easy to see how people could get so fat after the meal that she'd just had. Though it was weird. Despite how full she was, she still wanted to eat more. Though if she wasn't careful, she would probably end up as fat as Stephen. Or fatter. Still, it was an interesting thought....

As she walked, she imagined what it would be like to be so fat she was immobile, and had to have people feed her in bed as she continued to grow and grow and grow. She was still fantasizing about it as she walked into her house and was greeted by a mouth-watering aroma coming from the kitchen.

"Oh, Sam, you're just in time." Her mother said, poking her head out of the dining room, "I made a feast to celebrate you and your sister's first day!"

Sam glanced down at her swollen middle as it let out a growl of anticipation. Dropping her school supplies by the door, she rushed into the dining room where the rest of her family was already seated. She quickly took her seat and began devouring the meal like she hadn't eaten all day, until she finally leaned back in her seat, rubbing her very swollen belly.

She really didn't know what had come over her. Maybe it was just the stress of being in a new place, or maybe she just felt like she didn't have to keep up appearances since she didn't know anyone here really. However, she couldn't deny that part of her was enjoying the feeling of bingeing on all this food.

As she was getting ready for bed, she noticed her sister's door was cracked slightly, and she thought she heard moaning. She creeped towards the door, trying to be careful not to make any noise as she peered into her sister's room. Her eyes went wide as she saw her sister on her bed surrounded by snacks as one hand rubbed her chubby belly.

Her sister had never really seemed to care that she was fat, but this almost looked like she was enjoying it. Still, there was part of Sam that had always been jealous of Heather. She had always been chubby, and she never really seemed to care what other's thought of her. Part of her had always wished that they could switch places, but she'd always been too scared to let herself indulge like her sister.

Sam couldn't help watching her sister stuffing her belly on more snacks despite the large meal she'd just had, and she found herself leaning closer and closer to the crack in the door. Suddenly she lost her balance and fell forward, falling through the open door as her sister let out a small "Eep!"

"What are you doing?" Heather asked, jumping to her feet, which caused her whole body to jiggle.

"I, uh, I noticed the door was open and-"

"So you were spying on me?"

"Well, I, uh, yeah."

Heather shook her head as she sat back down on her bed, "Don't just lay there, close the door."

Sam closed the door behind her, watching her sister intently, "So, um."

Heather grabbed another cookie from a bag beside her, "You're probably wondering what I was doing."

Sam just nodded as she moved to sit on the side of the bed.

Heather sighed, rubbing her flabby belly, "I, um, I like being fat. And I've been so hungry today that I just couldn't help wanting to eat more. I've always wanted to be fatter than everyone else, but it was so hard to gain discretely back in our last city. But here, I don't really stick out. I want to get fatter, but you can't tell mom or dad. Who knows how they'd react. Please don't tell them."

Her sister couldn't keep the pleading tone from her voice with that last part, and she looked at Sam with a look of desperation. She honestly couldn't say she was surprised. Heather always seemed way to comfortable with her fat body, but Sam hadn't realized that she was trying to get fat on purpose. Though was that really much different than what she had been dreaming about all afternoon. If there was ever a place to indulge that fantasy, this was it. Still, she wasn't sure what would happen if she gave into this desire.

Of course the other option was to get her fill by watching her sister blow up like a balloon, and she could probably help that along. She had to admit that she'd always enjoyed her sister's fat body and her sister was admitting that it wasn't going anywhere. Of course she wasn't convinced she was really into this whole fat thing anyway, so maybe she should try to distance herself as much as possible from it.
111.title: Why do today...

choices: Get fast food
Go for a run
See if she can find Stephen at school
1111.content:
Sam smiled at her sister, grabbing a cookie from the bag, "Honestly, I was thinking about gaining some weight too. I figure it might be interesting to see what it's like. Though I don't think I'll get as fat as you."

Heather let out a sigh of relief, "I was worried you'd think I was weird or something. Or even worse, tell mom."

Sam grabbed another cookie, "Us sisters have to look out for each other. Especially since we're the new kids on the block."

Heather laid back on her bed, her chubby belly on clear display as she downed another cookie, "That's kind of what I'm thinking. This is a fresh start for us. No time like the present, especially since currently we don't exactly fit in with everyone else."

Sam shoved another cookie into her mouth, "It's definitely weird. Everything here is almost completely opposite from our home. Well, our old home."

Sam took a deep breath as she grabbed another cookie, though she couldn't help noticing the somber look on her sister's face either. It had been hard for both of them to move, and the more they talked about their old home, the more she missed it. And she knew her sister felt the same. Yet, despite the fact that they'd only been here a few days, there was almost a sense of normalcy coming to their lives.

Today was their first day of school, and even though everyone in town was obese, it still felt like school. Their new home was bigger, but it still felt like a home. Even the town felt similar despite having nothing but restaurants and a movie theater. Although, Sam had never really had a place to go hiking, and from what Stephen said, the forest had a bunch of great trails. Not like she'd be able to find anyone to go with her.

After a few more minutes of silence, Sam mentioned the movie theater in town, and the two of them began discussing what sort of movies would be playing in such a small town, and whether they'd be able to keep up on all the series they were used to. Eventually, the discussion changed to what other things they'd like to do in this new town, all while gorging themselves of the snacks Heather had snuck to her room. However, eventually Sam grew tired and decided to go back to her room so she could get some sleep.

Throughout the night, she kept dreaming of getting fatter and fatter. She grew to epic proportions as she ate and ate and ate. And yet, it all seemed so familiar to her, like something that had been with her for a while, and yet she'd forgotten about. She was happy though, happier than she'd been in a long time, and eventually her dreams changed.

She was young, probably 10 or 11, but it was hard to tell. She was in her room playing video games, her chubby belly protruding from her pajamas as she sat on the end of her bed. Morning light was streaming through the large window. Her friends had invited her to go hiking, but she didn't want to go. She just wanted to play video games on her day off. Still, her mother kept saying that was why she was gaining weight, and yet here she was, she was still playing video games, while she gorged herself on snacks. Honestly, she didn't really care. She actually kind of liked her soft belly. Besides, the rest of her family was fat too. Her mother. Her father. Even her sister were all fat. Though it wasn't like her mom really did much to force her to change.

She glanced at her door, which was slightly ajar. She could smell bacon cooking in the kitchen, and her mother singing off key to something on the radio. Despite the snacks she was eating, she was eager for a nice cooked breakfast. She smiled as she caught the sound of her sister snoring in the bed against the other wall, and she glanced over at the chubby girl.

She jumped as a beeping sound filled the air, and she cast her eyes about trying to find the source. Everything was dark, and yet the beeping filled the air. Her eyes bolted open, and she let out a sigh as she turned off her alarm clock. A faint blue glow filled the room, signalling early morning as she rolled out of bed. However, she hadn't even gone 2 steps when her stomach let out a loud growl. She almost doubled over as a huge wave of hunger washed over her.

She moved in front of the stand-mirror in her room, half expecting to see a chubby 10-year-old, and yet, even though she knew the truth, she still felt disappointed as she saw her toned reflection staring back at her. She lifted up her pink shirt, looking at her flat belly in the mirror. She'd almost hoped she'd look fatter after all the food she'd eaten yesterday, but these things took time. Still, it almost seemed like her belly was a little softer than it had been the day before, but that was probably just wishful thinking.

Wordlessly, she started getting ready for the day. After getting showered, she moved to the kitchen where her obese mother was just putting the finishing touches on a large breakfast. She couldn't help notice that even in her family, she was the odd one out. Where she was thin and athletic, the rest of them were definitely borderline huge.

Her mother was probably over 300 pounds, and loved to eat and cook. Which was good since her cooking was so delicious. Her dad was even bigger than her mother, and had always kind of reminded her of a young Santa Claus. Her sister had been one of the fattest girls in her class, though that wasn't too hard considering most of them had been pretty thin. Sam on the other hand, just didn't seem to fit in. That was going to change though. Starting today, she was going to work to put on weight and at least get chubby again.

She ate as much as she could before she had to be leaving for school. However, she still had enough time she could stop and grab some fast food before she had to be to class. Though, just because she wanted to put on weight, didn't mean she had to completely stop exercising. Especially if she wanted to be able to carry her larger body. She had time she could go for a quick run. Finally, she could just go to school and see if she could find Stephen. There were a few things she wanted to ask him about that he'd mentioned the day before.
1111.title: Fresh Start

choices: Go Hiking
Go out to eat with Stephen and Heather
Video game with Stephen and Heather
11111.content:
Despite her large breakfast, Sam couldn't deny that she was definitely still hungry. It was weird though how hungry she'd been lately. She'd never had this big an appetite before coming here. Though, it wasn't like she really had time to think about it if she wanted to grab some food before school.

Finishing getting ready, she stepped out into the cool morning air, as a breeze rustled the few red and orange leaves that were clinging to the trees. Shivering slightly, she sunk deeper into her jacket as she stuck off towards one of the fast food places that were relatively on the way.

As she walked she found herself chuckling slightly. This kind of reminded her of her old home, and how it used to be. Some mornings she'd leave early for school and stop at Henry's Coffee and pick up a doughnut and a hot cocoa on her way to class. Her friends would always tease her that she was going to get fat if she kept having doughnuts for breakfast, though she'd always reply that she was thinner than most of them.

She let out a sigh as she kicked at a stray leaf in her path. It was hard to deny that she still missed her old life. Though, maybe that was why she was so determined to be a different person here. Back home, her friends had always been into the latest fashions or diets. They all wanted to be star athletes, or runway models. But Sam hadn't. She'd been more into video games and computers, but those didn't make people like you. Those didn't stop people from teasing you. Back home, people cared more about how you looked, than who you were. And so Sam had made looks, who she was. But she couldn't deny there had always been something there that made her different.

She continued the rest of the journey, hardly paying attention to what was going on around her mind reminisced on her old life. Even ordering food was just a mindless task to her, though she eagerly began devouring the large quantity of food as she began moving towards her school. All the while, it was feeling like her shirt and jacket were getting tighter around her middle.

Finally arriving at school, she could see a few students still making their way to class. She quickly joined them and made her way inside. Today went by much the same as it did the day before, though she made sure to go back for seconds at lunch, which left her feeling uncomfortably full. Still, it already felt like she was starting to get into a routine. The other change was that she decided to sit by Stephen during the classes they had together, which there were only a couple. He definitely seemed happy to have someone to talk to, and she was happy to have someone making her feel so welcome.

The rest of the week went by in a blur, as she tried to get caught up on her lessons, and adjust to her new life. She found herself spending a lot of time eating as she studied, which was quite frequent given the amount of homework she needed to get caught up on since starting partway through the semester. She didn't really have any time to go jogging or work out either, but it wasn't like she had time to worry about that right now. Still, she was glad when the weekend finally came.

She slowly got out of bed, tugging on her shirt as it rode slightly up her middle. She was still fairly toned, but she couldn't deny that she was starting to seem a bit softer. However, it wasn't really enough for anyone else to tell yet. Now she needed to decide what to do after breakfast.

Still, she wasn't sure what she should do. It was a nice day so she could go hiking. Maybe she could even drag Stephen along with her. Though, he was more likely to want to go get some food or play video games. However, it wasn't like she'd be opposed to either of those. Maybe she could even drag Heather along.
11111.title: New You

choices: Investigate source of noise
Forget the noise, she's too hungry to sleep
She has enough will to ignore both, or does she
111112.content:
Sam twirled in front of the large mirror in her room, trying to ignore the smells of breakfast wafting from the kitchen. She was starving. Of course that seemed to be her default state lately. She couldn't believe how ravenous she'd been since arriving in Bountiful. Still, it did make it easier to achieve her new goal of putting on some weight. She had to admit, she was liking the results so far. Though, it was barely noticeable.

Her long black hair framed a beautiful face with bright green eyes. Her shirt barely bulged over her chest, reminding her of how much she was lacking in certain areas. Her gaze followed her shirt down to her middle, where she knew she was starting to show the most. She lifted up her shirt, revealing a relatively flat belly. However, she knew that she was losing definition in her abs beneath the thinnest layer of fat that was forming there, even if others didn't.

She smiled at what she saw. She knew it was weird, but she couldn't help being proud of the progress she'd made in just one week. She was getting excited to see what she'd look like in another 30 or 40 pounds. She kept telling herself she didn't want to get as fat as most people in town, but she definitely wanted to be unmistakably fat.

She couldn't resist anymore and tore her gaze away from the mirror, quickly making her way down to the kitchen where she knew her mother was preparing a large breakfast. She had to chuckle to herself; it seemed like her and her sister weren't the only ones more hungry lately.

Her mother was just setting a plate loaded with food at Sam's spot as she plopped down in her chair. Her stomach growled at the sight and smell of the hearty breakfast before her. She glanced across the table at her chubby sister who was already mostly through her meal.

"So," Sam said, swallowing a mouthful of food, "I was wondering if you wanted to go get lunch with me today with a couple friends?"

Heather glanced up from her meal just long enough to confirm that Sam was speaking to her, "Sure. That sounds fun!"

"Sam, It's so nice of you to be including your sister in things. I'm so proud of the two of you."

Sam found herself blushing as her mother pulled her into a side hug. Honestly, she'd completely forgot that her mother was even there.

"Mom!" Sam said, trying to pull away from her mother.

"Oh, alright, I'll leave the two of you alone." Her mother said, leaving the kitchen to finish getting ready for work.

Despite the large pay raise her father had gotten with his new job here, her mother had wanted to return to work. She claimed it was so she didn't get bored, but Sam wasn't entirely sure about that. Fortunately, she'd been able to get a job at a local diner as a cook, though Sam hadn't had the opportunity to try it yet. Maybe they'd have to hit it up for dinner since her mom was going to be working late tonight.

"So," Heather said, keeping her voice low, "Do I finally get to meet this Stephen who you keep hanging out with?"

"Yeah, I was thinking we could go to the buffet place with him. I was going to suggest a hike, but I don't really want to go alone."

"Besides, buffets are great!" Heather said, patting her soft belly, "They make it so easy to binge on all my favorite foods!"

Sam chuckled, admiring how tight Heather's shirt looked around her swollen middle. It seemed like Heather would need new clothes soon if she kept growing at the rate she was. She was like a bottomless stomach. Every time Sam saw her, she was eating. Still, she couldn't help admiring how cute and soft her sister was looking. She had nice round cheeks and a cute double chin which made her look almost like a 10-year-old, despite being 14. Her chest was also bigger than Sam's, which made an interesting contradiction with the apparent age of her face. Still, she couldn't deny that the biggest part of her sister was definitely her belly. It looked like someone had shoved a small tire into her shirt, though it was a lot more curvy than a tire, which made her look even more attractive. If she had to guess, her sister was probably close to, if not over, 200 pounds, which was significantly heavier than her own 130. Though now that she thought about it, she might be more now. She hadn't weighed herself since they'd arrived in Bountiful.

Sam and Heather finished their meal and went to get ready for the day. Fortunately, Stephen was up to having lunch and agreed to meet them at the buffet place. However, by the time she was finished getting ready for the day, Sam found she was already hungry again. She raided the snack cupboard, which was starting to look kind of bare, and retreated to Heather's room to play video games until it was time to go.

Finally, they met up with Stephen, and after introductions they began to feast on as much food as they could eat. Sam had been worried that Stephen and Heather wouldn't get along, but it seemed like she had nothing to worry about. They talked at length about video games, and the latest movies. Which fortunately, Sam was well versed in as well. Honestly, it was very freeing to be able to discuss her actual interests with someone without them thinking she was a geek or weird.

Once they'd all had their fill, they made their way to Stephen's house to try out a new game that had just released. His parents weren't home when they arrived, so he just grabbed a bunch of snacks for them, and instructed them to do the same. They spent the next few hours having fun and eating snacks to their heart's content, until it was finally time for Heather and Sam to be leaving.

They walked to the diner where their mother worked, hoping to get a nice big meal. Finally they arrived outside what Sam would describe as a generic diner, though it seemed packed with the diner crowd. They found an empty booth and a server quickly took their order. The portion sizes were immense, but Heather and Sam were more than up to the challenge.

That night as Sam was laying in bed rubbing her swollen belly, trying to find a comfortable position, she thought she heard someone move downstairs. She glanced at the clock, which showed almost midnight. Part of her wondered if her sister had gotten hungry again and went to get some more snacks. Honestly, she was kind of tempted to do the same. Despite how full she was, she was still hungry. She really was becoming such a glutton. She kept pushing her belly to its limits, and it was definitely showing in her appetite.

She opened her eyes as she heard the noise again. That sounded like it had come from below her room, not from the kitchen though. She was definitely curious to see who was up. Though her hunger was also calling to her. Though, she was tired enough that she was almost willing to ignore both and just go to sleep.
111112.title: Trend Setter

choices: Lie and say she's just been more relaxed since leaving the city
Tell the truth about how hungry she's been lately
Lie and blame it on hanging out with Stephen
Admit she's been trying to put on a bit of weight
1111122.content:
Letting out a small sigh, Sam threw her covers off herself. She shivered as the cool air tickled her skin, and she almost changed her mind. Still, she was sure she wouldn't be able to get any sleep until she got something to eat. Carefully, she crept out of her room and down towards the kitchen. She stopped at her sister's door, opening it slightly, she could make out her sister's large form sleeping soundly in bed.

She gently closed the door before continuing her trek to the kitchen. Finally, she made her way over to where her family kept the snacks and began gorging herself. Everything was so good, and yet it almost felt like a dream. She'd completely forgotten the noise she'd heard earlier as she stuffed herself with fatty foods.

She rubbed her chubby belly as she glanced towards the entrance to the kitchen. Her parents had told her not to eat any more snacks after dinner, but she just couldn't help it. It seemed like she was always hungry, and these snacks were the only thing that made her feel better. She knew it was just making the problem worse, but she couldn't help it.

The button on her pants had popped off during school, and everyone had started laughing at her. They called her a fat pig and other mean names, and yet the only thing that seemed to make it feel better was exactly what was causing it. She knew she was getting fat, but it didn't bug her. What hurt her was how the other kids treated her. Maybe she should work on losing weight. At least then they wouldn't keep bugging her. But it wasn't like being overweight bothered her.

One of her hands instinctively went to her side, sliding under her tight shirt to where she felt a little divot in her skin. It wasn't her first one, but it was her most recent. She'd had to look up what they were when her parents weren't around.

Sam jumped as something cold crinkled under her belly. She glanced around, taking in her bedroom around her. She didn't even remember returning to bed last night, but it looked like she'd brought half the snacks in the house with her. There were empty wrappers all around her, though that would explain what she was laying on. However, she was almost starting to get used to waking up like this.

Over the last couple of weeks she'd found herself sneaking down to the kitchen for snacks after everyone else had gone to bed, and more than once she'd woken up to find herself surrounded by wrappers. She couldn't help it. She just was always so hungry. Besides, she wasn't the only one who was snacking more than usual.

Heather was like an eating machine. She was growing fatter almost daily as she devoured more and more food. Sam loved watching her sister's clothes growing tighter and tighter as her belly grew larger and larger. She wished she could gain as fast as Heather, but part of her was glad she couldn't

She shoved the wrappers to the floor, laying on her back as she glanced at the clock. It was still pretty early, but later than she normally got up on a school day. Fortunately, today was Sunday so she didn't have to worry about that.

Sighing, she threw her covers off herself, again letting the cool air envelope her as she shivered slightly. The rest of the house was still quiet meaning everyone else was probably still sleeping. Which meant it was a perfect opportunity to go soak in the tub.

She pulled herself out of bed, tugging at her shirt as it squeezed tightly at her middle. The fabric felt tight, though that was normal. It was supposed to conform to her body after all. Still, she couldn't deny that her pajama bottoms were starting to feel a little tighter as well.

She knew she was gaining weight. She'd seen it herself. She hadn't expected to start gaining this quickly though, but she was actually kind of excited that she could.

Carefully, she picked her way through her wrapper covered floor and opened her door. Sure enough, it seemed like everyone else was sleeping still. Even Heather. She moved across the hall and quietly closed the bathroom door behind her. She turned on the light before stripping her clothes and starting the water in the tub.

She stopped as she was raising her leg over the edge of the tub, spotting something in the mirror. She stepped back moving closer to the light as she turned her eyes towards her side.

A small red mark was starting to form on her side, surrounded by the faded remains of other's of its kind. Something she hadn't seen or even thought about for a long time. Something from when she'd been younger and a lot less fit.

She examined herself in the mirror looking at the numerous red marks that were starting to form on her sides. She had looked it up, and figured out that they were called stretch marks. They formed when you put on weight so quickly that your skin couldn't keep up. The internet said they'd fade with time, but only if she stopped gaining weight. Otherwise, they'd just keep growing.

She knew she was getting fat, but she didn't realize that she could get fat too fast. Still, they almost looked kind of cute on her round belly. She hadn't shown anyone else since she didn't know how they'd react. However, she was sure she'd seen some on her sister's belly when they'd gone swimming last.

Sam's hand ran over the mark, a mixture of anxiety and glee filled her as she felt the slight divot in her skin. She couldn't believe she was starting to get a stretch mark. She knew she'd been gaining quickly, but she hadn't realized it had been that quickly. She wasn't sure how to feel. On the one hand, it definitely meant she was gaining weight. On the other it was something she hadn't been expecting.

Her once tone abs were definitely starting to disappear behind a layer of fat as she looked at her pudgy belly. It was getting harder and harder to conceal the fact that she was fattening up. She wouldn't even really call herself chubby, but there was no denying that she was getting thicker. Even her breasts were starting to grow a little, which really made her happy.

Sighing she tore her gaze away from the mirror and stepped into the hot water, letting it wash over her soft body. She couldn't help thinking about how much weight she had gained in just under a month. She had jumped from 130 up to 145. That was fifteen pounds in less than a month. At this rate she would be chubby in just a couple more months.

After almost an hour, she drained the tub and dried herself off, taking one last look at the newly forming stretch mark before putting her clothes back on. Her belly was starting to poke through between her bottoms and shirt, though it still looked really cute.

As she stepped out of the bathroom, she heard her father call from his bedroom, "Sam. Could you come here for a minute?"

Sam bit her lip, trying to think about what he could want as she made her way into her parent's room. Her mother wasn't there, and her dad was sitting in the armchair in the corner of the room with a book resting on his large belly.

"Yeah?"

"I've noticed that you and your sister seem to have been eating a lot more lately and it's starting to show." He said matter-of-factly, causing Sam to glance at her exposed belly as she felt her face reddening, "Now what you choose to do is up to you. I'm not going to stop you or tell you how to live your life. Heaven knows I don't have much room to talk, but I was wondering, have you been feeling unusually hungry lately? Like you don't have any control over it? I know that everyone in this town seems to be a bit heavier and sometimes it gets easy just to go with the flow, but do you feel like your hunger is abnormal?"

Sam bit her lip as she watched her father. He seemed to be watching her intently as she thought about how to answer.
1111122.title: Forget the noise, she's too hungry to sleep

choices: Go out to eat by herself
Go play video games with Stephen
See if Heather wants to go get some food with Stephen
Weight gain Porn in her room
Maybe her mom is right and she should at least try to stay fit
11111223.content:
Sam bit her lip, avoiding eye contact with her father as she thought about what to say. She didn't want to admit to him that there were times she felt like she wasn't in control. This morning being one of them. Yet, it almost seemed like he had guessed it anyway. Still, she didn't necessarily want to admit that to him.

"I hadn't really thought about it," Sam started, choosing her words carefully, "but, now that you mention it, I guess I have been eating more since hanging out with Stephen. It's like you said, just going with the flow."

She watched her father intently, waiting to see if he bought her half truth. It was true that she'd been eating more since hanging out with Stephen, but she knew that was only part of it.

"Alright. Well as long as you're happy, I guess that's what matters. I'm sorry to spring this on you, but your mother asked me to talk with you about it. She's worried that you're going to end up like everyone else in this town. I told her not to worry so much, and that you were old enough to make your own decisions, but you know how she gets." You father chuckled, picking his book back up, "Oh, and before I forget, I got called into the office today, and your mother has work so it will be just the two of you today."

Sam let out a quiet sigh of relief as she left her parents room. She knew her mother didn't really approve of her weight gain, but it seemed like she was trying to get her father to do the dirty work. She stomped towards the kitchen, not caring if she woke up her sister as she stormed past.

It looked like her mother had already left for work since she wasn't anywhere to be found, but Sam was okay with that. She didn't want to deal with her mother right now. She couldn't believe that she was trying to get her father involved in hounding her.

Sam stormed to one of the cupboards and grabbed an armful of food, while shoving a cookie in her mouth. It made her so angry. She knew her mother didn't approve, but she always told her and her sister that it was their choice. She shoved another cookie into her mouth, as she began unwrapping a cupcake. The food was helping her to calm down, just like it did when she was younger.

"You know you shouldn't eat just because you're upset." Sam's mom said, standing with her hands on her hips as she watched her daughter try to stash the bag of chips behind her.

"But it helps me feel better." replied the young, fat girl.

She felt her face go red as she struggled to resist putting another handful of chips into her mouth. Her shirt could no longer hide her growing belly, which rested heavily in her lap. The video game on the TV was paused, forgotten to her lust for food. She had recently gone from being just chubby to being fat. Still, she didn't care.

Sam took a deep breath, savoring the fatty food as she shoved more and more of it into her face. Each bite seemed to take away a little of her frustration as she lounged on her bed, devouring the snacks she'd grabbed. When she'd been younger she'd had a problem with eating whenever she was stressed or sad. It had always made her feel better.

She had kind of repressed that part of her life though. She had honestly forgotten that she used to be really fat at one point. She hadn't really cared about her weight back then. It wasn't until she'd gotten into middle school that she'd really lost a lot of weight. She had actually been one of the fattest kids in school when she was in elementary and even the first part of middle school. Still, it wasn't the weight that had bothered her. It was the other kids.

Now, there was no pressure to be thin or athletic. Honestly, the pressure was the other way now. She almost felt out of place by being skinny. Without that pressure to be thin though, it had definitely made it easier to go back to being that fat little child. She didn't really care if she was fat, honestly, she wanted that. Even as a child she'd enjoyed the feeling of having a soft body. That hadn't changed, and the bigger she got, the more she was enjoying it.

She threw the last empty bag to the floor. She felt quite a bit calmer, and yet, she still wanted more food. It just made her feel so good. Still, it wasn't like she'd been playing as many video games as she wanted, and she knew that Stephen had just picked up that new one that she wanted to try. The day was still early.
11111223.title: Lie and blame it on hanging out with Stephen

Raid the cabinet at home for snacks before bed
She's too tired, just head home to bed.
111112232.content:
Sam glanced around her messy room at the numerous wrappers that littered every surface available. Rolling her eyes she went to the kitchen and grabbed a trash bag and another package of cookies before heading back to her room. She began cleaning up all the wrappers and made her bed while devouring the cookies.

Nodding in satisfaction, she glanced around her room. It was tidy, though she wouldn't really say clean yet. At least her bed was made, and it didn't look like a complete slob lived there anymore. Still, there were a few clothes on the floor, and her desk was pretty cluttered, but that could wait until later. At least this way her mother wouldn't see all the snacks she'd eaten.

She picked out a shirt, and threw it on. It clung tightly to her body, emphasizing the slight belly that was starting to protrude from her middle. This was made even more apparent by how her tight pants gave her a muffin top. She really would need to pick up some more clothes. Maybe she could do that with Heather later.

She left her room, making her way downstairs she stopped just outside the kitchen to admire how big her sister was getting. Sam was almost jealous of how big her sister was getting, and with the way she was eating she wasn't going to stop anytime soon. Her belly rested heavily in her lap as it pressed slightly into the table. Heather's thick arms shoveled food into her mouth at an astonishing rate.

Heather's clothes looked several sizes too small on the obese girl. Her pants were undone, but still looked painfully tight around her wide waist. While her shirt didn't even cover her deep belly button, leaving quite a bit of her fat belly exposed. She had numerous stretchmarks on her soft skin, though Sam had to admit they looked good on her sister.

Feeling a flutter of excitement, she tore her gaze away from her massive sister. She couldn't help feeling jealous of her as she wished that was her. She wished she could be the girl who did nothing but eat all the time as she grew fatter and fatter. She wondered if Heather would get fat enough to become immobile, though at the rate she was growing that seemed like a valid possibility.

It wasn't like anyone really was judging her sister negatively. If anything, she seemed to fit in better than Sam in this town. She was really starting to look like one of the normal residents.

Taking one last look at the kitchen where she knew Heather was gorging herself, she stepped out into the cool morning air. It didn't take her long to walk to Stephen's house, though she was definitely chilly by the time she arrived. That would be one benefit of being fat. She would stay warmer.

She knocked on the door and waited. Finally, she heard the sounds of heavy footsteps coming from the other side of the door. A beaming face greeted her as the door opened, and the massive boy stepped aside to let her in, "Hey Sam."

"Hey. Thought I might come over to try that new game out." Sam said, admiring the massive boy as he closed the door behind her.

"You really just need to pick it up so we can play together," he chuckled, though he began leading her to his room, his footsteps sounding heavily through the house.

Sam felt a flutter in her chest as she watched him laboring on. His massive body shaking and jiggling with each step as he made his way in front of her. He seemed even bigger than when they'd first met, though from how much food he ate, it wasn't a surprise. Still, she couldn't deny that there was something about how big he was that excited her. Taking a deep breath she followed him to his room.

The two of them played games for hours, while Stephen made sure they both stayed stocked up on snacks. He even went so far as to keep feeding her while her hands were occupied with the controller. She didn't care though. She was having too much fun. She wished it could just go on like this forever, though like all good things eventually it had to come to an end.

It was later than she had expected when she finally left, stepping into the cold of the night. Despite how much she'd eaten today, she had to admit that she was still hungry. She wasn't sure if there would be any dinner waiting for her when she got home. Especially if Heather got to it. Still, it was pretty late and she kind of just wanted to get to bed. Though, she'd probably be too hungry to go to bed if she didn't get anything.
111112232.title: Go play video games with Stephen

choices: Give up her lunch and go check on him
Go after School
Wait. He's probably just sick
1111122321.content:
Sam shivered slightly as she pulled her jacket closer to her. Despite how late it was, the low hanging clouds kept it fairly light outside as she began making her way down the short driveway towards the sidewalk. She let her feet guide her through the cold night, as her stomach let out the occasional growl of hunger. It was nights like this that really made her wish she had a car.

Back in the city, it hadn't made much sense for her to have a car. They were expensive and there wasn't much parking, and what parking there was, was usually expensive. As such, she'd never really cared about getting a car despite the fact that she was 16. She had also been in better shape back then, and walking had just been an expected occurrence.

She pulled open the door to the restaurant, not even surprised by the size of the people inside. At first it had been weird to see so many fat people, but now it was almost normal.

Sam glanced down at her own belly which was well hidden by her jacket. Anyone that looked at her would think she was a twig. Though that wasn't the case anymore.

She looked around at the other people as she sat down with a massive tray of food. She didn't know why she'd ordered so much, but she eagerly began eating. She really didn't know what was coming over her.

Well, that wasn't entirely true. Without the pressure of her friends and society, she was reverting to how she had been when she was younger. She was reverting to the obese girl that struggled just to climb a flight of stairs.

She took in the people around her, admiring how confident they all seemed despite how fat they were. Her gaze stopped as she spied a girl in the corner of the lobby. She was about 11, and she was with a few friends who looked to be about the same age. The girl was gobbling up more food than Sam, while her friends were doing the same.

The girl was very obese, with far too small clothes. Her large, exposed belly rested heavily in her lap, covered in stretchmarks. She was definitely the fattest of her group of friends by probably 50 pounds. If Sam had to guess, she'd say the girl was probably close to, if not over, 200 lbs.

Sam bit her lip as she watched the girl eating. There had been a time when she'd been that big. Maybe even bigger. Though she hadn't been nearly as happy as that girl looked. However, it wasn't her size that had really made her unhappy. It was the way people treated her. She wondered if she would've been as big as Stephen by now if she'd have grown up here. Here were no one seemed to care if you were fat, she probably wouldn't have felt any need to lose the weight.

Instinctively, she stood up and ordered another tray of food as she continued to watch the girl laughing and having fun with her friends. She didn't even seem to care that her obese body was on display for the whole world as she continued feeding her growing belly.

Honestly, Sam probably would've fit in perfectly back when she was that girl's age. She probably would be even bigger than Stephen by now if she had grown up here. Food just made her feel so good. The feeling of being stuffed full was one of the best feelings she'd ever had. Food just made her feel so much better. She'd probably be immobile by now. Though apparently, even that wasn't frowned upon here.

Eventually, the kids got up and left. Sam watched the girl as she waddled after her friends, her belly swaying side-to-side as she walked. There was no doubt in Sam's mind that that girl was going to be huge in a couple years. She'd probably be immobile by the time she was Sam's age.

Sam finished the last of her feast, feeling how full she felt. She threw her garbage away and stepped outside into the cold. She let her mind wander to those years she'd tried to forget. To the times that seemed to be increasingly on her mind. A part of her regretted changing. Of losing the weight. She would've fit in so much better here if she hadn't. Still, part of her wished that she had grown up here. Wished that she could be that girl. Not even caring that she was growing fatter by the day.

She was still thinking about it when she got home. The smell of Lasagna wafted out from the kitchen, drawing her in. The rest of the house was dark, but the light above the sink was on, and she noticed a large plate of lasagna with her name on it.

She was still so full from her feast, but she didn't want to waste the food. She stuck it in the microwave for a minute, stopping it before the buzzer went off. She shoveled the food into her mouth with a ravenous hunger before finally going off to bed with an overstuffed belly.

Sam huffed as she climbed the stairs to school. She could feel the weight of her body slowing her down as even the simplest movement seemed difficult. Despite all the teasing, she was still getting bigger. If anything, she was growing faster now with all her midnight snacks. She stopped at the top of the stairs, exhausted.

She knew she was getting huge. She knew she was going to have an even harder time as she got fatter. It wasn't like she was trying to get fatter. It was just that food was so good. Still, most of the other kids were teasing her even more now, but that just lead to her eating more. It was a vicious circle. She knew she had to be the one to stop it, but it was just so hard.

She'd weighed herself that morning. She'd been about 210, though she wasn't very tall so she looked huge. She just kept growing and growing though. She was probably one of the fattest girl's her age, but she couldn't help it. She was struggling even to do some of the simplest things. Still, part of her loved the way it felt to be so big.

She'd often admire her growing body in the mirror in her room late at night. She'd imagine it growing bigger and bigger. She'd seen cartoons where the characters got so fat they couldn't move. Part of her wanted that. She couldn't deny it. Part of her wanted to be the girl who got so big she couldn't move. However, there was another part of her that wanted to fit in.

Sam sighed as her alarm went off. Groggily she turned it off, laying back on her bed. She'd been dreaming about the fattest she'd ever been. Back then she hadn't really realized just how big she was. She knew she was fat, but she hadn't realized that her size was incredibly uncommon. She hadn't realized that she was probably fatter than ninety-nine percent of the girls her age. Still, it was shortly after that she'd started losing weight.

Her stomach growled as the scent of breakfast wafted up from the kitchen. She could hear noises coming from her sister's room, which meant she was probably up too. Though that would make sense. They both had school today.

Sam rushed to get ready before heading down to breakfast. She ate as much as she could in the time allotted, though she grabbed a handful of bacon as she was walking out the door.

When she got to school, she sat down next to where Stephen usually sat, but by the time the first bell rang, he wasn't there. He wasn't in second period either. Or third. Finally it was time for lunch. She had long enough, she could probably go check and see if he was at home, though that would mean giving up her meal. Or she could go after school. The other option was just to wait until tomorrow. It was possible that he was just sick.

See what her sister is doing.
Go Clothes shopping
Look for a job at the fast food restaurant
Look for another job
11111223212.content:
Sam was too hungry to even think about giving up her lunch. Even for her best friend. Besides, it wasn't like he was in trouble or immediate danger. If there was one thing she'd realized here, it was that despite everyone's size, the usual health consequences didn't seem to apply. Honestly, the people here seemed healthier than even back in her last city.

The rest of the school day went by without much event. It was fairly boring, but Sam was finding herself doing well on her school work. She had always been more on the intelligent side. Though it was definitely less enjoyable without Stephen here.

Finally, the last bell rang and Sam grabbed her bag and rushed out. The sound of heavy students moving around surrounded her as she rushed out into the cool autumn air. The sounds of the students quickly faded behind her as she quickly walked down the sidewalk.

She still wasn't used to how quiet this town was once she got away from the school. Back at her last city, there was always the sound of traffic or people chatting. It was almost impossible to go more than a second without running into someone. Here, she could make the entire trek home without running into another person. There really weren't even that many cars. She might see five or ten cars on her whole trip home.

She walked past her house, continuing down a couple more streets until she arrived in front of Stephen's house. She made her way up the driveway and knocked on the door.

She waited for a few minutes until she finally heard the sound of heavy footsteps making their way towards the door. Slowly the door opened and she saw Stephen smiling at her, "Hey!"

He stepped aside, letting Sam into the warmth of the house, "Hey! You weren't at school today."

"Yeah, I don't know if you can tell, but I've put on weight since you came to town, and it's getting too hard for me to keep going to school. I was planning on telling you yesterday, but it completely slipped my mind." He said, looking sheepishly at the ground, "anyway we can talk about it more up in my room."

On the way to his room, they stopped in the kitchen to load up on snacks. Sam was so glad that his family kept so many snacks stocked up since she was already starving again. Stephen closed his door behind them as he moved to his bed. Sam took a seat on his large chair, breaking into a bag of chips.

"So, yeah, I decided to join the immobile student program." He said through a mouthful of food.

"But you're not immobile."

He chuckled, "Yeah, not yet, but I know it will probably happen soon. Technically anyone can join it, even you could. It's more just like student initiated homeschooling, where the school still is involved in your education. Most kids use it when they get too fat to go to school."

Sam looked at him with shock, "Wait! You mean I could just say that I want to join the program even though I'm super skinny?"

"Yeah, they got in trouble for discriminating a while back, and so anyone who asks can join it. You don't have to attend any classes unless your grades start to slip, then you just get enrolled in online classes that you have to participate in at a set time."

"That could be nice." Sam said, crumpling up the empty bag and opening another.

She was already forming a plan.
